channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Merging Methods for Multilingual Knowledge Editing for Large Language Models: An Empirical Odyssey
item.description: arXiv:2605.13919v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multilingual knowledge editing (MKE) remains challenging because language-specific edits interfere with one another, even when locate-then-edit methods work well in monolingual settings. This paper focuses on three issues: the effectiveness of vector ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, large language model, arxiv, vector, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: VectraYX-Nano: A 42M-Parameter Spanish Cybersecurity Language Model with Curriculum Learning and Native Tool Use
item.description: arXiv:2605.13989v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present VectraYX-Nano, a 41.95M-parameter decoder-only language model trained from scratch in Spanish for cybersecurity, with a Latin-American focus and native tool invocation via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Four contributions: (i) Corpus: Ve...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, transformer, LLM, release, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mistletoe: Stealthy Acceleration-Collapse Attacks on Speculative Decoding
item.description: arXiv:2605.14005v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speculative decoding has become a widely adopted technique for accelerating large language model (LLM) inference by drafting multiple candidate tokens and verifying them with a target model in parallel. Its efficiency, however, critically depends on t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, LLM, experiment, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Derivation Prompting: A Logic-Based Method for Improving Retrieval-Augmented Generation
item.description: arXiv:2605.14053v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The application of Large Language Models to Question Answering has shown great promise, but important challenges such as hallucinations and erroneous reasoning arise when using these models, particularly in knowledge-intensive, domain-specific tasks. ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, large language model, arxiv, augmented, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PEML: Parameter-efficient Multi-Task Learning with Optimized Continuous Prompts
item.description: arXiv:2605.14055v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) is widely used for adapting Large Language Models (LLMs) for various tasks. Recently, there has been an increasing demand for fine-tuning a single LLM for multiple tasks because it requires overall less data for ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, large language model, arxiv, LLM, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Distribution Corrected Offline Data Distillation for Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.14071v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Distilling reasoning traces from strong large language models into smaller ones is a promising route to improve intelligence in resource-constrained settings. Existing approaches face a fundamental trade-off: offline distillation from teacher-generate...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, vision, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Measuring and Mitigating Toxicity in Large Language Models: A Comprehensive Replication Study
item.description: arXiv:2605.14087v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs), when trained on web-scale corpora, inherently absorb toxic patterns from their training data. This leads to ``toxic degeneration'' where even innocuous prompts can trigger harmful outputs. This phenomenon poses significan...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, LLM, research, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When Evidence Conflicts: Uncertainty and Order Effects in Retrieval-Augmented Biomedical Question Answering
item.description: arXiv:2605.14115v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Biomedical retrieval-augmented large language models (LLMs) often face evidence that is incomplete, misleading, or internally contradictory, yet evaluation usually emphasizes answer accuracy under helpful context rather than reliability under conflict...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, augmented, LLM, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GraphBit: A Graph-based Agentic Framework for Non-Linear Agent Orchestration
item.description: arXiv:2605.13848v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic LLM frameworks that rely on prompted orchestration, where the model itself determines workflow transitions, often suffer from hallucinated routing, infinite loops, and non-reproducible execution. We introduce GraphBit, an engine-orchestrated f...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, augmented, LLM, prompt, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mixed Integer Goal Programming for Personalized Meal Optimization with User-Defined Serving Granularity
item.description: arXiv:2605.13849v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Determining what to eat to satisfy nutritional requirements is one of the oldest optimization problems in operations research, yet existing formulations have two persistent limitations: continuous variables produce impractical fractional servings (1.7...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, research, paper, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Two-Dimensional Framework for AI Agent Design Patterns: Cognitive Function and Execution Topology
item.description: arXiv:2605.13850v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing frameworks for LLM-based agent architectures describe systems from a single perspective: industry guides (Anthropic, Google, LangChain) focus on execution topology -- how data flows -- while cognitive science surveys focus on cognitive functi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, analysis, RAG, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Invisible Orchestrators Suppress Protective Behavior and Dissociate Power-Holders: Safety Risks in Multi-Agent LLM Systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.13851v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent orchestration -- in which a hidden coordinator manages specialized worker agents -- is becoming the default architecture for enterprise AI deployment, yet the safety implications of orchestrator invisibility have never been empirically tes...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, alignment, LLM, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PREPING: Building Agent Memory without Tasks
item.description: arXiv:2605.13880v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agent memory is typically constructed either offline from curated demonstrations or online from post-deployment interactions. However, regardless of how it is built, an agent faces a cold-start gap when first introduced to a new environment without an...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> demonstration, paper, arxiv, experiment, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PolitNuggets: Benchmarking Agentic Discovery of Long-Tail Political Facts
item.description: arXiv:2605.14002v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) embedded in agentic frameworks have transformed information retrieval from static, long context question answering into open-ended exploration. Yet real world use requires models to discover and synthesize "long-tail" fac...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, retrieval, framework, context, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From Descriptive to Prescriptive: Uncover the Social Value Alignment of LLM-based Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.14034v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Wide applications of LLM-based agents require strong alignment with human social values. However, current works still exhibit deficiencies in self-cognition and dilemma decision, as well as self-emotions. To remedy this, we propose a novel value-based...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> CoT, arxiv, alignment, LLM, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Vision-Based Runtime Monitoring under Varying Specifications using Semantic Latent Representations
item.description: arXiv:2605.13923v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study certified runtime monitoring of past-time signal temporal logic (ptSTL) from visual observations under partial observability. The monitor must infer safety-relevant quantities from images and provide finite-sample guarantees, while being \emp...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, vision, vector, image, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mechanistic Interpretability of EEG Foundation Models via Sparse Autoencoders
item.description: arXiv:2605.13930v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: EEG foundation models achieve state-of-the-art clinical performance, yet the internal computations driving their predictions remain opaque: a barrier to clinical trust. We apply TopK Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) across three architecturally distinct EEG...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, arxiv, transformer, model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Towards the Next Frontier of LLMs, Training on Private Data: A Cross-Domain Benchmark for Federated Fine-Tuning
item.description: arXiv:2605.13936v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The recent success of large language models (LLMs) has been largely driven by vast public datasets. However, the next frontier for LLM development lies beyond public data. Much of the world's most valuable information is private, especially in highly ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, paper, large language model, arxiv, platform | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: EvolveMem:Self-Evolving Memory Architecture via AutoResearch for LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.13941v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-term memory is essential for LLM agents that operate across multiple sessions, yet existing memory systems treat retrieval infrastructure as fixed: stored content evolves while scoring functions, fusion strategies, and answer-generation policies ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, retrieval, research, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Collider-Bench: Benchmarking AI Agents with Particle Physics Analysis Reproduction
item.description: arXiv:2605.13950v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous language-model agents are increasingly evaluated on long-horizon tool-use tasks, but existing benchmarks rarely capture the complexity and nuance of real scientific work. To address this gap, we introduce Collider-Bench, a benchmark for eva...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, arxiv, product, ICL, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Granite Embedding Multilingual R2: Open Apache 2.0 Multilingual Embeddings with 32K Context — Best Sub-100M Retrieval Quality
item.description: ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> Hugging Face Blog | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, embedding, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (Hugging Face Blog)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context window, prompt engineering, prompt, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, augmented, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> knowledge base, LLM, vector, retrieval, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.

item.title: Physics-R1: An Audited Olympiad Corpus and Recipe for Visual Physics Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2605.14040v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We audit the multimodal-physics evaluation pipeline end-to-end and document three undetected construction practices that distort how the field measures vision-language reasoning: train-eval contamination, translation drift, and MCQ saturation. (1) Pub...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> multimodal, arxiv, vision, LLM, release | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Conditional Attribute Estimation with Autoregressive Sequence Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.14004v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generative models are often trained with a next-token prediction objective, yet many downstream applications require the ability to estimate or control sequence-level properties. Next-token prediction can lead to overfitting of local patterns during t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, transformer, analysis, model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Sheaf-Theoretic Transport and Obstruction for Detecting Scientific Theory Shift in AI Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.14033v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scientific theory shift in AI agents requires more than fitting equations to data. An artificial scientific agent must detect whether an existing representational framework remains transportable into a new regime, or whether its language has become lo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, framework, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Enhanced and Efficient Reasoning in Large Learning Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.14036v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In current Large Language Models we can trust the production of smoothly flowing prose on the basis of the principles of machine learning. However, there is no comparably principled basis to justify trust in the content of the text produced. It appear...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, vision, product, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: EMA: Efficient Model Adaptation for Learning-based Systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.13942v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine learning (ML) is increasingly applied to optimize system performance in tasks such as resource management and network simulation. Unlike traditional ML tasks (e.g., image classification), networked systems often operate in heterogeneous, long-...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, arxiv, transformer, image, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, chain-of-thought, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: Rethinking Molecular OOD Generalization via Target-Aware Source Selection
item.description: arXiv:2605.13932v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Robust prediction of molecular properties under extreme out-of-distribution (OOD) scenarios is a pivotal bottleneck in AI-driven drug discovery. Current scaffold-splitting protocols fail to obstruct microscopic semantic overlap, predisposing models to...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, RAG, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond Mode-Seeking RL: Trajectory-Balance Post-Training for Diffusion Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.13935v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diffusion language models are a promising alternative to autoregressive models, yet post-training methods for them largely adapt reward-maximizing objectives. We identify a central failure mode in this setting we call trajectory locking: sampled rewar...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, RAG, model, prompt, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Unified Geometric Framework for Weighted Contrastive Learning
item.description: arXiv:2605.13943v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Contrastive learning (CL) aims to preserve relational structure between samples by learning representations that reflect a similarity graph. Yet, the geometry of the resulting embeddings remains poorly understood. Here we show that weighted InfoNCE ob...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, framework, embedding | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, step-by-step, prompt, prompt engineering, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Unsupervised learning of acquisition variability in structural connectomes via hybrid latent space modeling
